proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one continuous (float) feature in the dataset has a non-zero monotonic association with pfs_months.
Higher values of feature_078 are associated with longer pfs_months (positive Spearman correlation).

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
for c in cont_cols: stats.spearmanr(df[c], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman correlations of all 34 continuous features with pfs_months. Strongest positive: feature_078 rho=+0.853 (p<1e-300). Strongest negative: feature_013 rho=-0.223 (p<1e-300), feature_006 rho=-0.134 (p=4e-200). feature_009 rho=+0.118 (p=1e-154). Most other continuous features have |rho|<0.02 and are not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one binary (0/1) feature has a non-zero mean difference in pfs_months between groups.
Patients with feature_051=1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_051=0.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df[c]==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df[c]==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Welch t-tests across all 81 binary features. By far the largest effect is feature_051: mean pfs_months 3.509 (n=27,481) vs 4.031 (n=22,519); diff = -0.521 mo, p=1.6e-181. All other binary features show |diff|<0.10 mo and most are non-significant. feature_007 is constant (all zero) and was excluded.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057, an integer with three levels (0/1/2), shows a monotonic decreasing relationship with pfs_months consistent with disease stage.
feature_067 (Gleason-like, levels 6-10) is associated with shorter pfs_months at higher levels.

analyses.code: stats.spearmanr(df['feature_057'], df['pfs_months']); stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_057']==v,'pfs_months'] for v in [0,1,2]])
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_057: level 0 = 4.681 (n=17,592), level 1 = 3.492 (n=24,971), level 2 = 2.376 (n=7,437). Monotone decreasing as predicted. Spearman rho=-0.386, p<1e-300; one-way ANOVA F=4445.91, p<1e-300.

analyses.code: stats.spearmanr(df['feature_067'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_067 (5 levels: 6,7,8,9,10) shows essentially no relationship with pfs_months: rho=-0.0003, ANOVA F=1.07, p=0.37. Mean PFS is 3.71-3.76 across all five levels. Despite resembling Gleason in range, this feature is NOT prognostic in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Self-reported race (feature_018) is associated with mean pfs_months at the population level (any difference across racial categories).
Insurance category (feature_045) is associated with mean pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_018']==v,'pfs_months'] for v in df['feature_018'].unique()])
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by race: asian 3.797, black 3.741, hispanic 3.737, other 3.749, white 3.741. ANOVA F=0.56, p=0.69. No detectable race main effect on PFS.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_045']==v,'pfs_months'] for v in df['feature_045'].unique()])
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by insurance: medicaid 3.741, medicare 3.754, private 3.736, uninsured 3.726. ANOVA F=0.34, p=0.79. No detectable insurance main effect on PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a single-predictor OLS, each unit increase in feature_078 is associated with an increase in pfs_months on the order of 0.15-0.20 months (positive, large effect).
feature_009 has a positive linear effect on pfs_months on the order of +0.4 to +0.6 months per unit.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], sm.add_constant(df[['feature_078']])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS: pfs_months = -7.57 + 0.1742*feature_078. R^2=0.728. Beta=+0.174 mo per unit of feature_078, p<1e-300. feature_078 alone explains ~73% of variance in pfs_months.

analyses.code: sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], sm.add_constant(df[['feature_009']])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate OLS for feature_009: beta=+0.495 mo per unit, p=1.5e-166. Direction confirms hypothesis (positive, protective).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 has a long right tail (max 3622, median 16) and its association with pfs_months is better captured on a log scale than the raw scale.
Mean pfs_months decreases monotonically across quartiles of feature_013 from Q1 to Q4.

analyses.code: df['log_f013']=np.log1p(df['feature_013']); stats.pearsonr(df['log_f013'], df['pfs_months']); df.groupby(pd.qcut(df['feature_013'],4))['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Pearson(log_f013, pfs)=-0.231 (p<1e-300), tighter linear relationship than the raw feature (Spearman raw -0.223). Mean PFS by quartile of feature_013: Q1 4.311 -> Q2 3.933 -> Q3 3.618 -> Q4 3.115. Monotone decreasing as predicted. OLS slope on log scale: -0.382 mo per log-unit, p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_078 and pfs_months departs from linearity, with steeper slope at higher values (positive quadratic term).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + I(feature_078**2)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adding a quadratic term: beta_lin=+0.0735, beta_sq=+7.76e-04 (p_sq=1.9e-112). The positive quadratic indicates an upward curve: each additional unit of feature_078 buys progressively MORE pfs_months at the high end. Decile means rise from 0.85 mo (lowest decile, range 30-52) to 6.86 mo (highest decile, range 78-90), confirming non-linear acceleration.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, log(feature_013), feature_009, and feature_006, the apparent univariate effect of feature_051 on pfs_months attenuates substantially (toward zero), suggesting feature_051's effect is confounded by these covariates.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_009 + feature_006 + feature_051', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted multivariable OLS coefficients (R^2=0.969): feature_078 +0.174 (p<1e-300), feature_057=1 -1.178, feature_057=2 -2.286, log_f013 -0.383, feature_009 +0.478, feature_006 -0.077, feature_051 +0.0006 (p=0.87). The univariate -0.521 mo difference for feature_051 collapses entirely to zero after adjustment -- feature_051 is not an independent prognostic factor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_078 on pfs_months differs by level of feature_057 (interaction): the positive slope of feature_078 is shallower at higher stages.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 * C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction is significant: in stage 0 (reference), beta(feature_078)=+0.180 mo/unit; stage-1 interaction adds -0.0058 (p=4e-17), stage-2 interaction adds -0.0217 (p=4e-112). At feature_057=2, the effective slope of feature_078 is +0.158, ~12% shallower than stage 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, log(feature_013), feature_009, feature_006, and feature_051, race (feature_018) has no detectable effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_051 + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Race coefficients vs reference (asian): black +0.002 (p=0.78), hispanic +0.002 (p=0.83), other -0.0005 (p=0.97), white +0.003 (p=0.65). All race contrasts are essentially zero after adjustment. No racial disparity in main-effect PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment, insurance (feature_045) has no detectable effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_051 + C(feature_045)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Insurance coefficients vs reference (medicaid): medicare +0.0003 (p=0.95), private +0.0003 (p=0.95), uninsured +0.0008 (p=0.93). All effectively zero after adjustment. No insurance-related disparity in main-effect PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The univariate effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs by race (feature_051 x race interaction is non-zero).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 * C(feature_018) + feature_078 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms feature_051:race (vs asian reference): black +0.001 (p=0.98), hispanic -0.022 (p=0.45), other -0.054 (p=0.26), white -0.018 (p=0.48). None significant. The feature_051 effect is homogeneous across racial groups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The univariate effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs by insurance category (feature_051 x insurance interaction is non-zero).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 * C(feature_045) + feature_078 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms feature_051:insurance (vs medicaid reference): medicare -0.023 (p=0.19), private -0.025 (p=0.16), uninsured -0.014 (p=0.68). Not significant; feature_051 effect is homogeneous across insurance categories.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The reason feature_051's univariate effect vanishes on adjustment is that feature_051=1 patients have substantially higher feature_013 (and hence log_f013) than feature_051=0 patients; feature_051 is a marker for the high-feature_013 stratum.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_051']==1,'log_f013'], df.loc[df['feature_051']==0,'log_f013'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_051=1 vs 0 group means: log_f013 3.552 vs 2.175 (p<1e-300, huge difference); feature_078 64.98 vs 65.00 (p=0.82, balanced); feature_057 mean 0.796 vs 0.798 (p=0.77, balanced); feature_009 3.805 vs 3.798 (p=0.13); feature_006 3.818 vs 3.869 (p=0.14). The only covariate with strong imbalance is log_f013, confirming that feature_051 is acting as a surrogate for the high-feature_013 stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of log(feature_013) on pfs_months is attenuated (less steep) at higher levels of feature_057.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ log_f013 * C(feature_057) + feature_078 + feature_009 + feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: At feature_057=0, slope of log_f013 on PFS is -0.397; interaction with stage 1 is +0.014 (p=2e-6), with stage 2 is +0.052 (p=4e-38). Effective slope: stage 0 -0.397, stage 1 -0.383, stage 2 -0.345. Direction matches hypothesis: log_f013 effect is mildly attenuated at higher stages, but the magnitude of attenuation (~13% at stage 2) is small relative to the main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_009 on pfs_months is reduced at higher levels of feature_057.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_009 * C(feature_057) + feature_078 + log_f013 + feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: At feature_057=0, slope of feature_009 = +0.493; interaction with stage 1 is -0.011 (p=0.10, NS), with stage 2 is -0.060 (p=8e-10). At stage 2 the protective effect of feature_009 is ~12% smaller than at stage 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The slope of feature_078 on pfs_months differs across racial categories (feature_078 x race interaction is non-zero).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 * C(feature_018) + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_009 + feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_078 main slope (asian reference) +0.1750 mo/unit. Interactions vs asian: black -0.0020 (p=0.013), hispanic -0.0012 (p=0.11), other -0.0005 (p=0.71), white -0.0012 (p=0.07). Only the black-vs-asian contrast is significant, but the magnitude is tiny (~1% of the main effect). Functionally homogeneous slopes.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The slope of feature_078 on pfs_months differs across insurance categories.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 * C(feature_045) + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_009 + feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_078 slope at medicaid reference +0.1748. Interactions: medicare -0.0015 (p=0.003), private -0.0010 (p=0.05), uninsured -0.0008 (p=0.36). Magnitudes < 1% of main slope; clinically negligible despite statistical significance for medicare.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the established prognostic covariates (feature_078, feature_057, log_f013, feature_009, feature_006), at least one binary feature other than feature_051 has an independent effect on pfs_months on the order of >=0.10 months.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ {c} + feature_078 + log_f013 + feature_009 + feature_006 + C(feature_057)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Two binary features have large adjusted effects: feature_109 +0.153 mo (p=2e-183) and feature_039 +0.149 mo (p=5e-177). Both are protective and prevalence ~10%. The next-largest adjusted effect (feature_027) is only +0.025 mo, so feature_109 and feature_039 are clear outliers. feature_051's adjusted effect (+0.0006) confirms confounding diagnosis from iter 8.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The protective effect of feature_109 on pfs_months differs by stage (feature_057), by race (feature_018), and/or by insurance (feature_045).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109 * C(feature_057) + ...'); '... * C(feature_018) ...'; '... * C(feature_045) ...'
analyses.result_summary: feature_109 x stage: main +0.158 (stage 0), interaction with stage 1 -0.017 (p=0.14), stage 2 +0.020 (p=0.22). Effect is homogeneous across stages. feature_109 x race: all interaction p-values >0.5. feature_109 x insurance: medicare -0.026 (p=0.11), private -0.045 (p=0.006), uninsured -0.031 (p=0.28). Only the private-vs-medicaid contrast is significant, suggesting feature_109's main effect may be slightly weaker in privately insured patients (~25% reduction).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 and feature_039 act SYNERGISTICALLY: neither has an effect on pfs_months on its own, but patients who have BOTH show a markedly longer pfs_months (positive feature_109:feature_039 interaction term).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_009 + feature_006 + feature_109 * feature_039', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After full adjustment, feature_109 main effect = +0.0007 (p=0.90), feature_039 main effect = -0.0004 (p=0.94), but the feature_109:feature_039 interaction = +1.434 mo (p<1e-300). Unadjusted subgroup means: neither 3.742 mo (n=40,435), feature_039 only 3.668 (n=4,569), feature_109 only 3.671 (n=4,467), BOTH 5.216 (n=529). The combination delivers ~1.5 extra months of PFS that neither alone provides. Adjusted residual mean for the BOTH cell is +1.42 mo above zero. This is a textbook synergistic combination effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_109 x feature_039 synergy holds within each level of feature_057 (stage).

analyses.code: for s in [0,1,2]: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109 * feature_039 + feature_078 + log_f013 + feature_009 + feature_006', data=df[df['feature_057']==s]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within-stage interaction estimates: stage 0 +1.481 (p<1e-300, n=17,592), stage 1 +1.373 (p<1e-300, n=24,971), stage 2 +1.524 (p=4e-171, n=7,437). The synergy is large and consistent across stages, giving a roughly +1.4-1.5 mo absolute PFS bonus regardless of disease stage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The magnitude of the feature_109 x feature_039 synergistic benefit differs by self-reported race (three-way interaction is non-zero), with non-asian patients deriving a smaller benefit than asian patients.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109 * feature_039 * C(feature_018) + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_009 + feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction f109:f039:race (asian reference, synergy in asian = +1.625 mo): black -0.249 (p=0.002), hispanic -0.238 (p=0.002), white -0.199 (p=0.004), other +0.307 (p=0.03). Implied subgroup synergy magnitudes: asian +1.63, black +1.38, hispanic +1.39, white +1.43, other +1.93. Asian and 'other' patients derive ~15-20% MORE benefit from the combination than black, hispanic, or white patients. Direction supports hypothesis for black/hispanic/white vs asian, but 'other' goes the opposite way.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The magnitude of the feature_109 x feature_039 synergistic benefit differs by insurance category, with privately insured patients deriving a smaller benefit than medicaid patients.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109 * feature_039 * C(feature_045) + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_009 + feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Three-way f109:f039:insurance (medicaid reference, synergy in medicaid = +1.593 mo): medicare -0.132 (p=0.006), private -0.267 (p=3e-8), uninsured +0.004 (p=0.96). Implied subgroup synergies: medicaid +1.59, medicare +1.46, private +1.33, uninsured +1.60. Privately insured patients gain ~17% less from the combination than medicaid/uninsured patients; medicare is intermediate. Hypothesis supported for private vs medicaid contrast.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable model with the six main effects (feature_078, feature_057, log_f013, feature_009, feature_006, feature_109+feature_039 individually) plus a single feature_109 x feature_039 interaction term explains >=97% of variance in pfs_months and leaves no other binary feature with an adjusted effect comparable in magnitude.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + log_f013 + feature_009 + feature_006 + feature_109 * feature_039', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final model R^2 = 0.9744 (n=50,000). Significant coefficients: feature_078 +0.1736 mo/unit, feature_057=1 -1.178, feature_057=2 -2.288, log_f013 -0.383, feature_009 +0.479, feature_006 -0.0768, feature_109*feature_039 +1.434. feature_109 and feature_039 main effects ~0 (p>0.89). All other binary features have adjusted effects <0.03 mo and add negligible R^2. The model captures: a strong positive feature_078 effect, a stage gradient (feature_057), two unfavourable continuous biomarkers (log_f013, feature_006), one favourable continuous biomarker (feature_009), and a single combination synergy (feature_109+feature_039) operating only when both are present.
